COMPLETE CONDENSED MASTER ROADMAP (TOOLS 21-30)
TOOL 21: VISUAL COLLISION EDITOR
How Unity Does It
Unity Collision System:

Unity Collider Workflow:
1. Collider Components
   - Box Collider 2D (rectangle)
   - Circle Collider 2D (circle)
   - Polygon Collider 2D (custom shape)
   - Edge Collider 2D (line/chain)
   - Composite Collider 2D (merge multiple)

2. Visual Editing
   - Scene view gizmos (green outlines)
   - Drag handles to resize (Box/Circle)
   - Edit points for Polygon (add/remove/move vertices)
   - Offset property (move collider relative to object)
   - Material (physics material for friction/bounce)

3. Collision Detection
   - Trigger vs Collision mode
   - Layer-based collision matrix
   - Rigidbody interaction
   - Collision callbacks (OnCollisionEnter2D, etc.)

4. Debug Visualization
   - Collider gizmos in scene view
   - Physics.2D debugger
   - Collision contacts visualization
   - Raycast debugging
Key Unity Features:

Visual Gizmos: Green outlines showing collider shapes
Interactive Editing: Drag handles to modify shape
Polygon Editor: Click to add vertices, drag to move
Auto-Generation: Generate polygon from sprite
Composite Colliders: Merge adjacent colliders
Debug Draw: Show collision contacts/normals
Our Architecture
VisualCollisionEditor
├─── Collider Types
│    ├─── BoxCollider
│    │    ├─── Width/height properties
│    │    ├─── Offset (x, y)
│    │    └─── Rotation
│    ├─── CircleCollider
│    │    ├─── Radius property
│    │    └─── Offset (x, y)
│    ├─── PolygonCollider
│    │    ├─── Vertex list
│    │    ├─── Add/remove vertices
│    │    ├─── Drag vertices
│    │    └─── Auto-generate from sprite
│    └─── EdgeCollider
│         ├─── Point list
│         ├─── Chain/loop mode
│         └─── Thickness
│
├─── Visual Editing
│    ├─── Collider gizmos (overlay)
│    ├─── Resize handles (corners/edges)
│    ├─── Vertex handles (polygon)
│    ├─── Offset handle (center point)
│    └─── Rotation handle (arc)
│
├─── Collision Properties
│    ├─── Is Trigger (boolean)
│    ├─── Layer (collision layer)
│    ├─── Physics Material
│    │    ├─── Friction (0-1)
│    │    ├─── Bounciness (0-1)
│    │    └─── Density
│    └─── Enabled (toggle)
│
├─── Debug Visualization
│    ├─── Show all colliders
│    ├─── Highlight active colliders
│    ├─── Show collision contacts
│    ├─── Show physics bodies
│    └─── Color-coding (trigger vs solid)
│
├─── Auto-Generation
│    ├─── Generate from sprite bounds
│    ├─── Generate from alpha threshold
│    ├─── Simplify polygon (reduce vertices)
│    └─── Convex hull generation
│
└─── Integration
     ├─── Scene Editor integration
     ├─── Inspector panel (properties)
     ├─── Runtime collision system
     └─── Export to Phaser physics
Implementation
javascript
// tools/VisualCollisionEditor.js

class VisualCollisionEditor {
    constructor(editor) {
        this.editor = editor;
        this.scene = editor.scene;
        
        // Current editing state
        this.editMode = false;
        this.selectedCollider = null;
        this.selectedVertex = null;
        
        // Visualization
        this.showAllColliders = true;
        this.showContacts = false;
        
        // Gizmos layer
        this.gizmosLayer = this.scene.add.container(0, 0);
        this.gizmosLayer.setDepth(50000);
        
        this.initialize();
    }
    
    initialize() {
        console.log('[VisualCollisionEditor] Initialized');
    }
    
    // === COLLIDER CREATION ===
    
    addBoxCollider(sceneObject, width = 32, height = 32, offset = { x: 0, y: 0 }) {
        const collider = {
            type: 'box',
            width: width,
            height: height,
            offset: offset,
            rotation: 0,
            isTrigger: false,
            layer: 0,
            enabled: true,
            material: {
                friction: 0.5,
                bounciness: 0,
                density: 1.0
            }
        };
        
        sceneObject.addComponent('collider', collider);
        
        this.drawColliderGizmo(sceneObject, collider);
        
        return collider;
    }
    
    addCircleCollider(sceneObject, radius = 16, offset = { x: 0, y: 0 }) {
        const collider = {
            type: 'circle',
            radius: radius,
            offset: offset,
            isTrigger: false,
            layer: 0,
            enabled: true,
            material: {
                friction: 0.5,
                bounciness: 0,
                density: 1.0
            }
        };
        
        sceneObject.addComponent('collider', collider);
        
        this.drawColliderGizmo(sceneObject, collider);
        
        return collider;
    }
    
    addPolygonCollider(sceneObject, vertices = []) {
        // Default square if no vertices provided
        if (vertices.length === 0) {
            vertices = [
                { x: -16, y: -16 },
                { x: 16, y: -16 },
                { x: 16, y: 16 },
                { x: -16, y: 16 }
            ];
        }
        
        const collider = {
            type: 'polygon',
            vertices: vertices,
            offset: { x: 0, y: 0 },
            isTrigger: false,
            layer: 0,
            enabled: true,
            material: {
                friction: 0.5,
                bounciness: 0,
                density: 1.0
            }
        };
        
        sceneObject.addComponent('collider', collider);
        
        this.drawColliderGizmo(sceneObject, collider);
        
        return collider;
    }
    
    // === AUTO-GENERATION ===
    
    generateFromSprite(sceneObject) {
        const spriteComponent = sceneObject.getComponent('sprite');
        
        if (!spriteComponent || !spriteComponent.data.texture) {
            console.error('[CollisionEditor] No sprite to generate from');
            return null;
        }
        
        const texture = this.scene.textures.get(spriteComponent.data.texture);
        const source = texture.source[0];
        
        // Get sprite bounds
        const width = source.width;
        const height = source.height;
        
        // Generate polygon from alpha channel
        const vertices = this.traceAlphaOutline(texture, 128); // Alpha threshold
        
        if (vertices.length > 0) {
            return this.addPolygonCollider(sceneObject, vertices);
        } else {
            // Fallback to box
            return this.addBoxCollider(sceneObject, width, height);
        }
    }
    
    traceAlphaOutline(texture, alphaThreshold) {
        // Get pixel data
        const canvas = texture.getSourceImage();
        const tempCanvas = document.createElement('canvas');
        tempCanvas.width = canvas.width;
        tempCanvas.height = canvas.height;
        
        const ctx = tempCanvas.getContext('2d');
        ctx.drawImage(canvas, 0, 0);
        
        const imageData = ctx.getImageData(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height);
        const data = imageData.data;
        
        // Find opaque pixels
        const opaquePixels = [];
        
        for (let y = 0; y < canvas.height; y++) {
            for (let x = 0; x < canvas.width; x++) {
                const index = (y * canvas.width + x) * 4;
                const alpha = data[index + 3];
                
                if (alpha >= alphaThreshold) {
                    opaquePixels.push({ x, y });
                }
            }
        }
        
        if (opaquePixels.length === 0) return [];
        
        // Find convex hull
        const hull = this.convexHull(opaquePixels);
        
        // Convert to local coordinates (centered)
        const centerX = canvas.width / 2;
        const centerY = canvas.height / 2;
        
        return hull.map(point => ({
            x: point.x - centerX,
            y: point.y - centerY
        }));
    }
    
    convexHull(points) {
        // Graham scan algorithm for convex hull
        if (points.length < 3) return points;
        
        // Find lowest point
        let lowest = points[0];
        for (const p of points) {
            if (p.y > lowest.y || (p.y === lowest.y && p.x < lowest.x)) {
                lowest = p;
            }
        }
        
        // Sort by polar angle
        const sorted = points.slice().sort((a, b) => {
            const angleA = Math.atan2(a.y - lowest.y, a.x - lowest.x);
            const angleB = Math.atan2(b.y - lowest.y, b.x - lowest.x);
            return angleA - angleB;
        });
        
        // Build hull
        const hull = [sorted[0], sorted[1]];
        
        for (let i = 2; i < sorted.length; i++) {
            while (hull.length >= 2 && this.ccw(hull[hull.length - 2], hull[hull.length - 1], sorted[i]) <= 0) {
                hull.pop();
            }
            hull.push(sorted[i]);
        }
        
        return hull;
    }
    
    ccw(a, b, c) {
        return (b.x - a.x) * (c.y - a.y) - (b.y - a.y) * (c.x - a.x);
    }
    
    simplifyPolygon(vertices, tolerance = 2) {
        // Douglas-Peucker algorithm
        if (vertices.length <= 2) return vertices;
        
        // Find furthest point
        let maxDist = 0;
        let maxIndex = 0;
        
        const start = vertices[0];
        const end = vertices[vertices.length - 1];
        
        for (let i = 1; i < vertices.length - 1; i++) {
            const dist = this.pointLineDistance(vertices[i], start, end);
            if (dist > maxDist) {
                maxDist = dist;
                maxIndex = i;
            }
        }
        
        if (maxDist > tolerance) {
            // Recurse
            const left = this.simplifyPolygon(vertices.slice(0, maxIndex + 1), tolerance);
            const right = this.simplifyPolygon(vertices.slice(maxIndex), tolerance);
            
            return left.slice(0, -1).concat(right);
        } else {
            return [start, end];
        }
    }
    
    pointLineDistance(point, lineStart, lineEnd) {
        const dx = lineEnd.x - lineStart.x;
        const dy = lineEnd.y - lineStart.y;
        
        const num = Math.abs(dy * point.x - dx * point.y + lineEnd.x * lineStart.y - lineEnd.y * lineStart.x);
        const den = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);
        
        return num / den;
    }
    
    // === VISUAL EDITING ===
    
    enterEditMode(sceneObject, collider) {
        this.editMode = true;
        this.selectedCollider = { object: sceneObject, collider: collider };
        
        this.drawEditGizmos();
    }
    
    exitEditMode() {
        this.editMode = false;
        this.selectedCollider = null;
        this.clearEditGizmos();
    }
    
    drawEditGizmos() {
        this.clearEditGizmos();
        
        if (!this.selectedCollider) return;
        
        const { object, collider } = this.selectedCollider;
        
        if (collider.type === 'box') {
            this.drawBoxEditGizmos(object, collider);
        } else if (collider.type === 'circle') {
            this.drawCircleEditGizmos(object, collider);
        } else if (collider.type === 'polygon') {
            this.drawPolygonEditGizmos(object, collider);
        }
    }
    
    drawBoxEditGizmos(object, collider) {
        const worldX = object.transform.x + collider.offset.x;
        const worldY = object.transform.y + collider.offset.y;
        
        // Draw resize handles at corners
        const handles = [
            { x: -collider.width / 2, y: -collider.height / 2, cursor: 'nwse-resize' },
            { x: collider.width / 2, y: -collider.height / 2, cursor: 'nesw-resize' },
            { x: collider.width / 2, y: collider.height / 2, cursor: 'nwse-resize' },
            { x: -collider.width / 2, y: collider.height / 2, cursor: 'nesw-resize' }
        ];
        
        handles.forEach((handle, index) => {
            const handleGraphics = this.scene.add.graphics();
            handleGraphics.fillStyle(0xffaa00, 1);
            handleGraphics.fillCircle(worldX + handle.x, worldY + handle.y, 5);
            
            // Make interactive
            const hitArea = new Phaser.Geom.Circle(worldX + handle.x, worldY + handle.y, 8);
            handleGraphics.setInteractive(hitArea, Phaser.Geom.Circle.Contains, { 
                draggable: true, 
                useHandCursor: true 
            });
            
            handleGraphics.on('drag', (pointer, dragX, dragY) => {
                this.resizeBoxCollider(collider, index, dragX - worldX, dragY - worldY);
            });
            
            this.gizmosLayer.add(handleGraphics);
        });
        
        // Center offset handle
        const centerHandle = this.scene.add.graphics();
        centerHandle.fillStyle(0x4488ff, 1);
        centerHandle.fillCircle(worldX, worldY, 6);
        
        const centerHitArea = new Phaser.Geom.Circle(worldX, worldY, 10);
        centerHandle.setInteractive(centerHitArea, Phaser.Geom.Circle.Contains, {
            draggable: true,
            useHandCursor: true
        });
        
        centerHandle.on('drag', (pointer, dragX, dragY) => {
            collider.offset.x = dragX - object.transform.x;
            collider.offset.y = dragY - object.transform.y;
            this.drawEditGizmos();
        });
        
        this.gizmosLayer.add(centerHandle);
    }
    
    resizeBoxCollider(collider, handleIndex, localX, localY) {
        // Resize based on which corner is dragged
        switch (handleIndex) {
            case 0: // Top-left
                collider.width = Math.abs(collider.width / 2 - localX) * 2;
                collider.height = Math.abs(collider.height / 2 - localY) * 2;
                break;
            case 1: // Top-right
                collider.width = Math.abs(-collider.width / 2 - localX) * 2;
                collider.height = Math.abs(collider.height / 2 - localY) * 2;
                break;
            case 2: // Bottom-right
                collider.width = Math.abs(-collider.width / 2 - localX) * 2;
                collider.height = Math.abs(-collider.height / 2 - localY) * 2;
                break;
            case 3: // Bottom-left
                collider.width = Math.abs(collider.width / 2 - localX) * 2;
                collider.height = Math.abs(-collider.height / 2 - localY) * 2;
                break;
        }
        
        this.drawEditGizmos();
    }
    
    drawCircleEditGizmos(object, collider) {
        const worldX = object.transform.x + collider.offset.x;
        const worldY = object.transform.y + collider.offset.y;
        
        // Radius handle (on circle edge)
        const handleX = worldX + collider.radius;
        const handleY = worldY;
        
        const handle = this.scene.add.graphics();
        handle.fillStyle(0xffaa00, 1);
        handle.fillCircle(handleX, handleY, 5);
        
        const hitArea = new Phaser.Geom.Circle(handleX, handleY, 8);
        handle.setInteractive(hitArea, Phaser.Geom.Circle.Contains, {
            draggable: true,
            useHandCursor: true
        });
        
        handle.on('drag', (pointer, dragX, dragY) => {
            const dx = dragX - worldX;
            const dy = dragY - worldY;
            collider.radius = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);
            this.drawEditGizmos();
        });
        
        this.gizmosLayer.add(handle);
        
        // Center offset handle
        const centerHandle = this.scene.add.graphics();
        centerHandle.fillStyle(0x4488ff, 1);
        centerHandle.fillCircle(worldX, worldY, 6);
        
        const centerHitArea = new Phaser.Geom.Circle(worldX, worldY, 10);
        centerHandle.setInteractive(centerHitArea, Phaser.Geom.Circle.Contains, {
            draggable: true,
            useHandCursor: true
        });
        
        centerHandle.on('drag', (pointer, dragX, dragY) => {
            collider.offset.x = dragX - object.transform.x;
            collider.offset.y = dragY - object.transform.y;
            this.drawEditGizmos();
        });
        
        this.gizmosLayer.add(centerHandle);
    }
    
    drawPolygonEditGizmos(object, collider) {
        const worldX = object.transform.x + collider.offset.x;
        const worldY = object.transform.y + collider.offset.y;
        
        // Vertex handles
        collider.vertices.forEach((vertex, index) => {
            const handle = this.scene.add.graphics();
            handle.fillStyle(0xffaa00, 1);
            handle.fillCircle(worldX + vertex.x, worldY + vertex.y, 5);
            
            const hitArea = new Phaser.Geom.Circle(worldX + vertex.x, worldY + vertex.y, 8);
            handle.setInteractive(hitArea, Phaser.Geom.Circle.Contains, {
                draggable: true,
                useHandCursor: true
            });
            
            handle.on('drag', (pointer, dragX, dragY) => {
                vertex.x = dragX - worldX;
                vertex.y = dragY - worldY;
                this.drawEditGizmos();
                this.drawColliderGizmo(object, collider);
            });
            
            // Right-click to delete vertex
            handle.on('pointerdown', (pointer) => {
                if (pointer.rightButtonDown() && collider.vertices.length > 3) {
                    collider.vertices.splice(index, 1);
                    this.drawEditGizmos();
                    this.drawColliderGizmo(object, collider);
                }
            });
            
            this.gizmosLayer.add(handle);
        });
        
        // Add vertex button (click on edge)
        // This would be more complex - show "+" icons on edges
    }
    
    clearEditGizmos() {
        this.gizmosLayer.removeAll(true);
    }
    
    // === VISUALIZATION ===
    
    drawColliderGizmo(object, collider) {
        if (!this.showAllColliders) return;
        
        const worldX = object.transform.x + collider.offset.x;
        const worldY = object.transform.y + collider.offset.y;
        
        const gizmo = this.scene.add.graphics();
        
        const color = collider.isTrigger ? 0x00ff00 : 0x44ff88;
        const alpha = collider.enabled ? 0.6 : 0.3;
        
        gizmo.lineStyle(2, color, alpha);
        
        if (collider.type === 'box') {
            gizmo.strokeRect(
                worldX - collider.width / 2,
                worldY - collider.height / 2,
                collider.width,
                collider.height
            );
        } else if (collider.type === 'circle') {
            gizmo.strokeCircle(worldX, worldY, collider.radius);
        } else if (collider.type === 'polygon') {
            if (collider.vertices.length > 0) {
                gizmo.beginPath();
                gizmo.moveTo(worldX + collider.vertices[0].x, worldY + collider.vertices[0].y);
                
                for (let i = 1; i < collider.vertices.length; i++) {
                    gizmo.lineTo(worldX + collider.vertices[i].x, worldY + collider.vertices[i].y);
                }
                
                gizmo.closePath();
                gizmo.strokePath();
            }
        }
        
        this.gizmosLayer.add(gizmo);
    }
    
    refreshAllGizmos() {
        this.gizmosLayer.removeAll(true);
        
        this.editor.sceneObjects.forEach(obj => {
            const collider = obj.getComponent('collider');
            if (collider) {
                this.drawColliderGizmo(obj, collider.data);
            }
        });
    }
    
    toggleVisualization() {
        this.showAllColliders = !this.showAllColliders;
        
        if (this.showAllColliders) {
            this.refreshAllGizmos();
        } else {
            this.gizmosLayer.removeAll(true);
        }
    }
    
    // === EXPORT ===
    
    exportToPhaser(sceneObject) {
        const collider = sceneObject.getComponent('collider');
        
        if (!collider) return null;
        
        const data = collider.data;
        
        // Convert to Phaser physics format
        const phaserCollider = {
            type: data.type,
            offset: data.offset,
            isTrigger: data.isTrigger,
            enabled: data.enabled
        };
        
        if (data.type === 'box') {
            phaserCollider.width = data.width;
            phaserCollider.height = data.height;
        } else if (data.type === 'circle') {
            phaserCollider.radius = data.radius;
        } else if (data.type === 'polygon') {
            phaserCollider.vertices = data.vertices.map(v => [v.x, v.y]);
        }
        
        return phaserCollider;
    }
}

export default VisualCollisionEditor;
```

---

## TOOL 22: HOT RELOAD SYSTEM

### How Unity Does It

**Unity Hot Reload**:
```
Unity Live Reloading:
1. Script Recompilation
   - Detects C# file changes
   - Recompiles in background
   - Preserves game state (Domain Reload optional)
   - Serialization of values across recompile

2. Asset Importing
   - File watcher on Assets folder
   - Auto-reimport on changes
   - Texture/audio/prefab updates
   - Scene reloads automatically

3. Play Mode
   - Enter/Exit Play Mode options
   - Fast Enter Play Mode (skip domain/scene reload)
   - Serialize play mode changes (optional)
   - Scene auto-refresh

4. Editor Scripts
   - [ExecuteInEditMode] for preview
   - OnValidate() for property changes
   - Custom inspectors update live
```

### Our Architecture
```
HotReloadSystem
├─── File Watchers
│    ├─── Code files (.js)
│    ├─── Assets (sprites, audio)
│    ├─── Data files (.json)
│    └─── Scene files
│
├─── Module Reloading
│    ├─── Detect changed modules
│    ├─── Clear module cache
│    ├─── Re-import modules
│    ├─── Update class instances
│    └─── Preserve state
│
├─── Asset Reloading
│    ├─── Texture reload
│    ├─── Audio reload
│    ├─── JSON data reload
│    └─── Sprite atlas refresh
│
├─── State Preservation
│    ├─── Serialize current state
│    ├─── Game state snapshot
│    ├─── Object references map
│    └─── Restore after reload
│
└─── Browser Integration
     ├─── WebSocket connection
     ├─── File server communication
     ├─── Auto-refresh trigger
     └─── Error reporting
Implementation
javascript
// systems/HotReloadSystem.js

class HotReloadSystem {
    constructor(scene) {
        this.scene = scene;
        
        // WebSocket connection to dev server (if available)
        this.socket = null;
        this.connected = false;
        
        // File change tracking
        this.watchedFiles = new Map();
        this.lastModified = new Map();
        
        // State preservation
        this.savedState = null;
        
        // Config
        this.config = {
            enabled: true,
            preserveState: true,
            autoReconnect: true,
            watchAssets: true,
            watchCode: true
        };
        
        this.initialize();
    }
    
    initialize() {
        if (this.config.enabled) {
            this.connectToDevServer();
        }
        
        console.log('[HotReloadSystem] Initialized');
    }
    
    // === DEV SERVER CONNECTION ===
    
    connectToDevServer() {
        try {
            // Connect to local dev server (e.g., Vite, Webpack)
            this.socket = new WebSocket('ws://localhost:5173'); // Vite default
            
            this.socket.onopen = () => {
                this.connected = true;
                console.log('[HotReload] Connected to dev server');
            };
            
            this.socket.onmessage = (event) => {
                this.handleMessage(event.data);
            };
            
            this.socket.onerror = (error) => {
                console.warn('[HotReload] WebSocket error:', error);
            };
            
            this.socket.onclose = () => {
                this.connected = false;
                console.log('[HotReload] Disconnected from dev server');
                
                if (this.config.autoReconnect) {
                    setTimeout(() => this.connectToDevServer(), 3000);
                }
            };
        } catch (error) {
            console.warn('[HotReload] Could not connect to dev server:', error);
        }
    }
    
    handleMessage(data) {
        try {
            const message = JSON.parse(data);
            
            if (message.type === 'update') {
                this.handleFileUpdate(message.path, message.content);
            } else if (message.type === 'full-reload') {
                this.fullReload();
            }
        } catch (error) {
            console.error('[HotReload] Message handling error:', error);
        }
    }
    
    // === FILE WATCHING (Browser-based fallback) ===
    
    watchFile(path, callback) {
        this.watchedFiles.set(path, callback);
        
        // Poll for changes (if no WebSocket)
        if (!this.connected) {
            this.pollFile(path);
        }
    }
    
    async pollFile(path) {
        try {
            const response = await fetch(path, { method: 'HEAD' });
            const lastModified = response.headers.get('Last-Modified');
            
            const previousModified = this.lastModified.get(path);
            
            if (previousModified && lastModified !== previousModified) {
                // File changed
                const callback = this.watchedFiles.get(path);
                if (callback) {
                    callback(path);
                }
            }
            
            this.lastModified.set(path, lastModified);
            
            // Continue polling
            setTimeout(() => this.pollFile(path), 2000);
        } catch (error) {
            console.warn('[HotReload] Poll error:', error);
        }
    }
    
    // === FILE UPDATE HANDLING ===
    
    handleFileUpdate(path, content) {
        const ext = path.split('.').pop();
        
        if (ext === 'js') {
            this.reloadJavaScript(path, content);
        } else if (['png', 'jpg', 'jpeg', 'gif', 'webp'].includes(ext)) {
            this.reloadTexture(path);
        } else if (['mp3', 'ogg', 'wav'].includes(ext)) {
            this.reloadAudio(path);
        } else if (ext === 'json') {
            this.reloadJSON(path);
        }
    }
    
    async reloadJavaScript(path, content) {
        console.log(`[HotReload] Reloading JavaScript: ${path}`);
        
        if (this.config.preserveState) {
            this.saveState();
        }
        
        try {
            // Clear module from cache
            if (window.moduleCache) {
                delete window.moduleCache[path];
            }
            
            // Re-import module
            const module = await import(path + '?t=' + Date.now());
            
            // Update instances (if applicable)
            this.updateModuleInstances(path, module);
            
            if (this.config.preserveState) {
                this.restoreState();
            }
            
            console.log(`[HotReload] Reloaded: ${path}`);
        } catch (error) {
            console.error(`[HotReload] Failed to reload ${path}:`, error);
        }
    }
    
    async reloadTexture(path) {
        console.log(`[HotReload] Reloading texture: ${path}`);
        
        // Get texture key from path
        const key = path.split('/').pop().split('.')[0];
        
        if (this.scene.textures.exists(key)) {
            // Remove old texture
            this.scene.textures.remove(key);
            
            // Load new texture
            await new Promise((resolve) => {
                this.scene.load.image(key, path + '?t=' + Date.now());
                this.scene.load.once('complete', resolve);
                this.scene.load.start();
            });
            
            // Update all sprites using this texture
            this.updateSprites(key);
            
            console.log(`[HotReload] Reloaded texture: ${key}`);
        }
    }
    
    async reloadAudio(path) {
        console.log(`[HotReload] Reloading audio: ${path}`);
        
        const key = path.split('/').pop().split('.')[0];
        
        if (this.scene.cache.audio.exists(key)) {
            this.scene.cache.audio.remove(key);
            
            await new Promise((resolve) => {
                this.scene.load.audio(key, path + '?t=' + Date.now());
                this.scene.load.once('complete', resolve);
                this.scene.load.start();
            });
            
            console.log(`[HotReload] Reloaded audio: ${key}`);
        }
    }
    
    async reloadJSON(path) {
        console.log(`[HotReload] Reloading JSON: ${path}`);
        
        try {
            const response = await fetch(path + '?t=' + Date.now());
            const data = await response.json();
            
            // Trigger reload event
            this.scene.events.emit('json-reloaded', path, data);
            
            console.log(`[HotReload] Reloaded JSON: ${path}`);
        } catch (error) {
            console.error(`[HotReload] Failed to reload JSON ${path}:`, error);
        }
    }
    
    // === STATE PRESERVATION ===
    
    saveState() {
        this.savedState = {
            scene: this.scene.scene.key,
            camera: {
                x: this.scene.cameras.main.scrollX,
                y: this.scene.cameras.main.scrollY,
                zoom: this.scene.cameras.main.zoom
            },
            objects: [],
            gameState: {}
        };
        
        // Save game objects
        this.scene.children.list.forEach(obj => {
            if (obj.getData) {
                this.savedState.objects.push({
                    type: obj.type,
                    x: obj.x,
                    y: obj.y,
                    data: obj.getData('state')
                });
            }
        });
        
        // Save custom game state
        if (this.scene.gameState) {
            this.savedState.gameState = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(this.scene.gameState));
        }
        
        console.log('[HotReload] State saved');
    }
    
    restoreState() {
        if (!this.savedState) return;
        
        // Restore camera
        this.scene.cameras.main.setScroll(
            this.savedState.camera.x,
            this.savedState.camera.y
        );
        this.scene.cameras.main.setZoom(this.savedState.camera.zoom);
        
        // Restore game state
        if (this.scene.gameState && this.savedState.gameState) {
            Object.assign(this.scene.gameState, this.savedState.gameState);
        }
        
        console.log('[HotReload] State restored');
    }
    
    // === UPDATE HELPERS ===
    
    updateModuleInstances(path, module) {
        // Find all instances of classes from this module
        // Update their prototypes
        
        // This is highly dependent on your architecture
        // Example: Update all enemies if Enemy class changed
        
        const moduleName = path.split('/').pop().split('.')[0];
        
        if (window.classRegistry && window.classRegistry[moduleName]) {
            window.classRegistry[moduleName] = module.default || module;
        }
    }
    
    updateSprites(textureKey) {
        // Find all sprites using this texture and refresh them
        this.scene.children.list.forEach(child => {
            if (child.type === 'Image' || child.type === 'Sprite') {
                if (child.texture.key === textureKey) {
                    const frame = child.frame.name;
                    child.setTexture(textureKey, frame);
                }
            }
        });
    }
    
    // === FULL RELOAD ===
    
    fullReload() {
        console.log('[HotReload] Full reload triggered');
        
        if (this.config.preserveState) {
            this.saveState();
            localStorage.setItem('hotreload_state', JSON.stringify(this.savedState));
        }
        
        window.location.reload();
    }
    
    // === PUBLIC API ===
    
    enable() {
        this.config.enabled = true;
        if (!this.connected) {
            this.connectToDevServer();
        }
    }
    
    disable() {
        this.config.enabled = false;
        if (this.socket) {
            this.socket.close();
        }
    }
    
    setPreserveState(preserve) {
        this.config.preserveState = preserve;
    }
}

export default HotReloadSystem;
Dev Server Integration (Vite Plugin Example):

javascript
// vite-plugin-phaser-hotreload.js

export function phaserHotReload() {
    return {
        name: 'phaser-hot-reload',
        
        configureServer(server) {
            // Watch for file changes
            server.watcher.on('change', (path) => {
                // Send update to connected clients
                server.ws.send({
                    type: 'custom',
                    event: 'phaser-update',
                    data: {
                        type: 'update',
                        path: path,
                        timestamp: Date.now()
                    }
                });
            });
        },
        
        handleHotUpdate({ file, server }) {
            // Determine if full reload needed
            if (file.endsWith('.html')) {
                server.ws.send({
                    type: 'custom',
                    event: 'phaser-update',
                    data: { type: 'full-reload' }
                });
            }
        }
    };
}
```

## TOOL 23: BUILD PIPELINE & OPTIMIZATION

### How Unity Does It

**Unity Build System**:
```
Unity Build Pipeline:
1. Build Settings
   - Platform selection (PC, Mobile, WebGL)
   - Scene inclusion list
   - Development/Release builds
   - Compression options

2. Player Settings
   - Company/Product name
   - Version number
   - Icons/splash screens
   - Resolution settings
   - Quality settings per platform

3. Asset Processing
   - Texture compression (Platform-specific)
   - Audio compression
   - Sprite atlas generation
   - Asset bundle creation
   - Code stripping

4. Optimization
   - Occlusion culling
   - Static batching
   - Dynamic batching
   - GPU instancing
   - LOD (Level of Detail)

5. Output
   - Executable/Package
   - Data files
   - Streaming assets
   - Build report (size breakdown)
```

### Our Architecture
```
BuildPipeline
├─── Configuration
│    ├─── Build profiles (dev, staging, production)
│    ├─── Platform targets (web, desktop, mobile)
│    ├─── Compression settings
│    └─── Output paths
│
├─── Asset Processing
│    ├─── Texture optimization
│    │    ├─── Format conversion (WebP, AVIF)
│    │    ├─── Resolution scaling
│    │    ├─── Compression levels
│    │    └─── Atlas packing
│    ├─── Audio optimization
│    │    ├─── Format conversion (MP3, OGG)
│    │    ├─── Bitrate reduction
│    │    └─── Sample rate adjustment
│    ├─── Code optimization
│    │    ├─── Minification
│    │    ├─── Tree shaking
│    │    ├─── Code splitting
│    │    └─── Bundle optimization
│    └─── Data optimization
│         ├─── JSON minification
│         ├─── Remove unused assets
│         └─── Asset references cleanup
│
├─── Build Steps
│    ├─── Pre-build validation
│    ├─── Asset compilation
│    ├─── Code bundling
│    ├─── Resource packing
│    ├─── Optimization passes
│    └─── Post-build verification
│
├─── Platform-Specific
│    ├─── Web
│    │    ├─── Service worker generation
│    │    ├─── Progressive Web App manifest
│    │    ├─── Asset preloading
│    │    └─── Browser compatibility
│    ├─── Desktop (Electron)
│    │    ├─── Platform binaries
│    │    ├─── Auto-updater
│    │    └─── Native modules
│    └─── Mobile (Capacitor/Cordova)
│         ├─── App icons/splash screens
│         ├─── Platform configs
│         └─── Native plugins
│
└─── Build Reporting
     ├─── Asset size breakdown
     ├─── Bundle analysis
     ├─── Performance metrics
     ├─── Dependency graph
     └─── Optimization suggestions
Implementation
javascript
// tools/BuildPipeline.js

class BuildPipeline {
    constructor() {
        this.profiles = {
            development: {
                minify: false,
                sourceMap: true,
                compression: 'none',
                optimization: 'none'
            },
            staging: {
                minify: true,
                sourceMap: true,
                compression: 'gzip',
                optimization: 'basic'
            },
            production: {
                minify: true,
                sourceMap: false,
                compression: 'brotli',
                optimization: 'aggressive'
            }
        };
        
        this.currentProfile = 'development';
        
        // Build output
        this.buildDir = 'dist';
        this.assetsDir = 'dist/assets';
        
        // Asset registry
        this.assets = {
            textures: [],
            audio: [],
            data: [],
            code: []
        };
        
        // Build statistics
        this.stats = {
            totalSize: 0,
            assetSizes: {},
            buildTime: 0
        };
    }
    
    // === BUILD ORCHESTRATION ===
    
    async build(profileName = 'production') {
        console.log(`[BuildPipeline] Starting ${profileName} build...`);
        
        this.currentProfile = profileName;
        const profile = this.profiles[profileName];
        
        const startTime = Date.now();
        
        try {
            // 1. Pre-build validation
            await this.validate();
            
            // 2. Clean build directory
            await this.clean();
            
            // 3. Scan assets
            await this.scanAssets();
            
            // 4. Process assets
            await this.processAssets(profile);
            
            // 5. Bundle code
            await this.bundleCode(profile);
            
            // 6. Generate manifests
            await this.generateManifests(profile);
            
            // 7. Post-build optimization
            await this.optimize(profile);
            
            // 8. Generate report
            this.stats.buildTime = Date.now() - startTime;
            await this.generateReport();
            
            console.log(`[BuildPipeline] Build completed in ${this.stats.buildTime}ms`);
            
            return {
                success: true,
                stats: this.stats
            };
        } catch (error) {
            console.error('[BuildPipeline] Build failed:', error);
            return {
                success: false,
                error: error.message
            };
        }
    }
    
    async validate() {
        console.log('[BuildPipeline] Validating project...');
        
        // Check required files
        const requiredFiles = [
            'index.html',
            'src/main.js',
            'public/assets'
        ];
        
        // Validate scene references
        // Check for missing assets
        // Verify configuration
    }
    
    async clean() {
        console.log('[BuildPipeline] Cleaning build directory...');
        
        // Remove existing build files
        // In Node.js, use fs.rmSync(this.buildDir, { recursive: true })
    }
    
    async scanAssets() {
        console.log('[BuildPipeline] Scanning assets...');
        
        // Scan public/assets directory
        // Build asset registry
        
        this.assets.textures = [
            // { path: 'assets/sprites/player.png', size: 12345 }
        ];
        
        this.assets.audio = [
            // { path: 'assets/audio/bgm.mp3', size: 123456 }
        ];
    }
    
    // === ASSET PROCESSING ===
    
    async processAssets(profile) {
        console.log('[BuildPipeline] Processing assets...');
        
        await this.processTextures(profile);
        await this.processAudio(profile);
        await this.processData(profile);
    }
    
    async processTextures(profile) {
        console.log('[BuildPipeline] Optimizing textures...');
        
        for (const texture of this.assets.textures) {
            // Load image
            const img = await this.loadImage(texture.path);
            
            // Optimize based on profile
            if (profile.optimization === 'aggressive') {
                // Convert to WebP
                const webp = await this.convertToWebP(img, 80);
                await this.saveFile(`${this.assetsDir}/${texture.name}.webp`, webp);
                
                // Generate multiple resolutions (@2x, @1x, @0.5x)
                await this.generateResolutions(img, texture.name);
            } else {
                // Just copy
                await this.copyFile(texture.path, `${this.assetsDir}/${texture.name}`);
            }
        }
    }
    
    async convertToWebP(imageData, quality) {
        // Use canvas to convert to WebP
        const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');
        canvas.width = imageData.width;
        canvas.height = imageData.height;
        
        const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');
        ctx.drawImage(imageData, 0, 0);
        
        return new Promise((resolve) => {
            canvas.toBlob((blob) => {
                resolve(blob);
            }, 'image/webp', quality / 100);
        });
    }
    
    async generateResolutions(imageData, name) {
        const resolutions = [
            { suffix: '@2x', scale: 1.0 },
            { suffix: '@1x', scale: 0.5 },
            { suffix: '@0.5x', scale: 0.25 }
        ];
        
        for (const res of resolutions) {
            const canvas = document.createElement('canvas');
            canvas.width = imageData.width * res.scale;
            canvas.height = imageData.height * res.scale;
            
            const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');
            ctx.drawImage(imageData, 0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height);
            
            const blob = await new Promise((resolve) => {
                canvas.toBlob(resolve, 'image/webp', 0.8);
            });
            
            await this.saveFile(`${this.assetsDir}/${name}${res.suffix}.webp`, blob);
        }
    }
    
    async processAudio(profile) {
        console.log('[BuildPipeline] Optimizing audio...');
        
        for (const audio of this.assets.audio) {
            if (profile.optimization === 'aggressive') {
                // Convert to OGG Vorbis (smaller than MP3)
                // Reduce bitrate to 128kbps
                // const optimized = await this.convertAudio(audio.path, 'ogg', 128);
            }
            
            // Copy to build dir
            await this.copyFile(audio.path, `${this.assetsDir}/${audio.name}`);
        }
    }
    
    async processData(profile) {
        console.log('[BuildPipeline] Processing data files...');
        
        for (const data of this.assets.data) {
            const content = await this.loadJSON(data.path);
            
            if (profile.minify) {
                // Minify JSON (remove whitespace)
                const minified = JSON.stringify(content);
                await this.saveFile(`${this.assetsDir}/${data.name}`, minified);
            } else {
                await this.copyFile(data.path, `${this.assetsDir}/${data.name}`);
            }
        }
    }
    
    // === CODE BUNDLING ===
    
    async bundleCode(profile) {
        console.log('[BuildPipeline] Bundling code...');
        
        // This would integrate with Vite, Webpack, Rollup, etc.
        // Example Vite integration:
        
        const viteConfig = {
            build: {
                outDir: this.buildDir,
                minify: profile.minify ? 'terser' : false,
                sourcemap: profile.sourceMap,
                rollupOptions: {
                    output: {
                        manualChunks: {
                            phaser: ['phaser'],
                            vendor: ['lodash', 'rxjs']
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        };
        
        // Run Vite build
        // await vite.build(viteConfig);
    }
    
    // === MANIFESTS ===
    
    async generateManifests(profile) {
        console.log('[BuildPipeline] Generating manifests...');
        
        // PWA manifest
        const pwaManifest = {
            name: 'Verdance',
            short_name: 'Verdance',
            start_url: '/',
            display: 'fullscreen',
            background_color: '#000000',
            theme_color: '#44ff88',
            icons: [
                { src: 'icon-192.png', sizes: '192x192', type: 'image/png' },
                { src: 'icon-512.png', sizes: '512x512', type: 'image/png' }
            ]
        };
        
        await this.saveFile(`${this.buildDir}/manifest.json`, JSON.stringify(pwaManifest, null, 2));
        
        // Service worker
        await this.generateServiceWorker(profile);
        
        // Asset manifest (for preloading)
        const assetManifest = {
            version: Date.now(),
            assets: [
                ...this.assets.textures.map(a => a.path),
                ...this.assets.audio.map(a => a.path),
                ...this.assets.data.map(a => a.path)
            ]
        };
        
        await this.saveFile(`${this.buildDir}/assets.json`, JSON.stringify(assetManifest));
    }
    
    async generateServiceWorker(profile) {
        const sw = `
// Service Worker for Verdance
const CACHE_NAME = 'verdance-v${Date.now()}';
const ASSETS = ${JSON.stringify(this.getAllAssetPaths())};

self.addEventListener('install', (event) => {
    event.waitUntil(
        caches.open(CACHE_NAME).then((cache) => {
            return cache.addAll(ASSETS);
        })
    );
});

self.addEventListener('fetch', (event) => {
    event.respondWith(
        caches.match(event.request).then((response) => {
            return response || fetch(event.request);
        })
    );
});
        `;
        
        await this.saveFile(`${this.buildDir}/sw.js`, sw);
    }
    
    getAllAssetPaths() {
        return [
            '/',
            '/index.html',
            ...this.assets.textures.map(a => '/' + a.path),
            ...this.assets.audio.map(a => '/' + a.path)
        ];
    }
    
    // === OPTIMIZATION ===
    
    async optimize(profile) {
        console.log('[BuildPipeline] Running optimizations...');
        
        if (profile.optimization === 'aggressive') {
            // Tree shaking (remove unused code)
            await this.treeShake();
            
            // Bundle splitting (code splitting)
            await this.splitBundles();
            
            // Asset compression
            await this.compressAssets(profile.compression);
        }
    }
    
    async compressAssets(method) {
        if (method === 'gzip' || method === 'brotli') {
            console.log(`[BuildPipeline] Compressing assets with ${method}...`);
            
            // Compress all text-based files (.js, .css, .html, .json)
            // In Node.js, use zlib for gzip, brotli-size for brotli
        }
    }
    
    async treeShake() {
        // Remove unused exports
        // This is typically handled by bundler (Vite, Webpack)
    }
    
    async splitBundles() {
        // Split into multiple bundles for lazy loading
        // Vendor chunk, Game code chunk, Scene chunks
    }
    
    // === REPORTING ===
    
    async generateReport() {
        console.log('[BuildPipeline] Generating build report...');
        
        const report = {
            profile: this.currentProfile,
            buildTime: this.stats.buildTime,
            totalSize: this.calculateTotalSize(),
            breakdown: {
                code: this.calculateCodeSize(),
                textures: this.calculateTextureSize(),
                audio: this.calculateAudioSize(),
                data: this.calculateDataSize()
            },
            assets: {
                textures: this.assets.textures.length,
                audio: this.assets.audio.length,
                data: this.assets.data.length
            },
            recommendations: this.generateRecommendations()
        };
        
        await this.saveFile(`${this.buildDir}/build-report.json`, JSON.stringify(report, null, 2));
        
        console.log('Build Report:', report);
        
        return report;
    }
    
    calculateTotalSize() {
        // Sum all file sizes
        return this.calculateCodeSize() +
               this.calculateTextureSize() +
               this.calculateAudioSize() +
               this.calculateDataSize();
    }
    
    calculateCodeSize() {
        // Get size of all .js files in build dir
        return 0; // Placeholder
    }
    
    calculateTextureSize() {
        return this.assets.textures.reduce((sum, texture) => sum + texture.size, 0);
    }
    
    calculateAudioSize() {
        return this.assets.audio.reduce((sum, audio) => sum + audio.size, 0);
    }
    
    calculateDataSize() {
        return this.assets.data.reduce((sum, data) => sum + data.size, 0);
    }
    
    generateRecommendations() {
        const recommendations = [];
        
        // Check for large textures
        const largeTextures = this.assets.textures.filter(t => t.size > 500000); // 500KB
        if (largeTextures.length > 0) {
            recommendations.push({
                type: 'warning',
                message: `${largeTextures.length} textures exceed 500KB. Consider compression or resolution reduction.`,
                files: largeTextures.map(t => t.path)
            });
        }
        
        // Check for unoptimized audio
        const largeAudio = this.assets.audio.filter(a => a.size > 2000000); // 2MB
        if (largeAudio.length > 0) {
            recommendations.push({
                type: 'warning',
                message: `${largeAudio.length} audio files exceed 2MB. Consider bitrate reduction.`,
                files: largeAudio.map(a => a.path)
            });
        }
        
        return recommendations;
    }
    
    // === FILE UTILITIES ===
    
    async loadImage(path) {
        return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
            const img = new Image();
            img.onload = () => resolve(img);
            img.onerror = reject;
            img.src = path;
        });
    }
    
    async loadJSON(path) {
        const response = await fetch(path);
        return await response.json();
    }
    
    async saveFile(path, content) {
        // In browser, use File System Access API
        // In Node.js, use fs.writeFile
        console.log(`[BuildPipeline] Saving ${path}`);
    }
    
    async copyFile(from, to) {
        // In Node.js, use fs.copyFile
        console.log(`[BuildPipeline] Copying ${from} -> ${to}`);
    }
}

export default BuildPipeline;

TOOL 24: PERFORMANCE PROFILER
How Unity Does It
Unity Profiler:

Unity Profiler Features:
1. CPU Profiler
   - Timeline view of frame execution
   - Hierarchy view (call stacks)
   - Function timing (ms per call)
   - GC allocations tracking
   - Deep profiling mode

2. GPU Profiler
   - Draw calls per frame
   - SetPass calls (state changes)
   - Triangles/vertices rendered
   - Texture memory usage
   - Shader compilation time

3. Memory Profiler
   - Heap snapshots
   - Object allocation tracking
   - Memory leaks detection
   - Native/managed memory breakdown
   - Asset memory usage

4. Rendering Profiler
   - Batching statistics
   - Overdraw visualization
   - Culling statistics
   - Shadow rendering cost
   - Post-processing cost

5. Physics Profiler
   - Active rigidbodies
   - Collision pairs
   - Raycast count
   - Physics update time

6. Audio Profiler
   - Active sounds
   - DSP CPU usage
   - Memory per clip
Key Unity Features:

Real-time Graphs: CPU/GPU/Memory over time
Frame Debugger: Step through draw calls
Deep Profiling: Per-function timing
Timeline View: Visualize parallel execution
Comparison: Compare two profiles
Platform Profiling: Profile on device
