```
## PASTE THIS EXACT PROMPT

### Complete "Starter Pack" Request
```
I'm building Verdance, a 2D tactical RPG in Phaser 3. I need to implement 5 essential development tools to create a professional workflow similar to Unity.

PROJECT CONTEXT:
- Game: Verdance (Phaser 3, JavaScript)
- Current State: Week 16 complete (combat, enemies, equipment)
- Goal: Add professional development toolkit

IMPLEMENT THESE 5 TOOLS:

1. DATA-DRIVEN ARCHITECTURE (Tool 1)
File: src/systems/DataDrivenSystem.js
Purpose: Load game data from JSON files instead of hardcoding
Interface:
  - async loadData(dataType, id) - Load JSON file
  - getData(dataType, id) - Get cached data
  - getAllData(dataType) - Get all data of type
Features:
  - Caching system
  - Async loading
  - Type organization (enemies/, items/, quests/)

2. SAVE/LOAD SYSTEM (Tool 14)
File: src/systems/SaveSystem.js
Purpose: Persist game state to localStorage
Interface:
  - registerCollector(key, collectFn, restoreFn)
  - save(slot) - Save to slot (1-5)
  - load(slot) - Load from slot
  - quickSave() / quickLoad()
Features:
  - Collector pattern (modular save data)
  - Multiple save slots
  - Autosave support

3. PREFAB SYSTEM (Tool 20)
File: src/systems/PrefabSystem.js
Purpose: Template-based GameObject instantiation
Interface:
  - register(id, template) - Register prefab
  - spawn(id, x, y) - Create instance
  - createPrefab(sceneObject, name) - Convert to prefab
Features:
  - JSON-based templates
  - Component-based data
  - Instance tracking

4. PERFORMANCE PROFILER (Tool 24)
File: src/tools/PerformanceProfiler.js
Purpose: Real-time performance monitoring
Interface:
  - enable() / disable()
  - mark(name) / measure(name, start, end)
  - HUD display
Features:
  - FPS counter
  - Memory usage (if available)
  - Draw call count
  - Frame timing
  - Toggle with F1 key

5. ADDRESSABLES SYSTEM (Tool 26)
File: src/systems/AddressablesSystem.js
Purpose: Asset loading by string address
Interface:
  - async loadAsset(address) - Load by address
  - registerAsset(address, type, path)
  - loadBundle(bundleId) - Load asset group
Features:
  - String-based addresses (e.g., "enemies.goblin")
  - Asset bundles for batch loading
  - Reference counting
  - Memory management

PROJECT STRUCTURE:
verdance/
  src/
    systems/
      DataDrivenSystem.js       ← CREATE
      SaveSystem.js             ← CREATE
      PrefabSystem.js           ← CREATE
      AddressablesSystem.js     ← CREATE
    tools/
      PerformanceProfiler.js    ← CREATE
    scenes/
      GameScene.js              ← UPDATE (show integration)
  public/
    data/
      enemies/
        goblin.json             ← CREATE EXAMPLE
    assets/
      asset_manifest.json       ← CREATE

DELIVERABLES:
1. All 5 system files with complete implementations
2. Example data files (goblin.json, asset_manifest.json)
3. Integration code for GameScene.js showing:
   - How to initialize all systems
   - How to use each system
   - Keyboard shortcuts (F1, F5, F9)
4. Brief usage documentation in comments

Please create production-ready, fully-functional code. Include error handling, JSDoc comments, and examples.

```
## SIDE-BY-SIDE EXAMPLE

### Creating DataDrivenSystem.js

**In Cursor:**
```
You: "Create src/systems/DataDrivenSystem.js with this code..."

Cursor:
1. Shows you inline diff

```
## WHEN TO USE WHICH TOOL

### Use Cursor When:
```
✓ You're editing existing code
✓ You want real-time suggestions AS you type
✓ You need to refactor existing functions
✓ You want inline code completion
✓ You're making small, iterative changes

Example: "Fix this bug in my combat system"
         "Refactor this function to be more efficient"
         "Add error handling to this method"
```

### Use Claude Code When:
```

✓ You're creating NEW systems from scratch       ← YOUR SITUATION

```
### 2. Create All Systems (30 minutes)
```
Cmd+K (Composer)

"Create these 5 development systems for my Phaser 3 game:

FILE: src/systems/DataDrivenSystem.js
```javascript
class DataDrivenSystem {
    constructor(scene) {
        this.scene = scene;
        this.cache = new Map();
    }
    
    async loadData(dataType, id) {
        const response = await fetch(`/data/${dataType}/${id}.json`);
        const data = await response.json();
        this.cache.set(`${dataType}.${id}`, data);
        return data;
    }
    
    getData(dataType, id) {
        return this.cache.get(`${dataType}.${id}`);
    }
    
    getAllData(dataType) {
        const results = [];
        this.cache.forEach((value, key) => {
            if (key.startsWith(dataType + '.')) {
                results.push(value);
            }
        });
        return results;
    }
}
export default DataDrivenSystem;
```

[Paste the other 4 systems...]

Also create example files:
- public/data/enemies/goblin.json
- Integration code for GameScene.js

Review the changes in the sidebar, accept all.

wat if cursor doesn’t have context .. how do i give cursor context

Feb 8
HOW TO GIVE CURSOR FULL CONTEXT
CURSOR'S CONTEXT SYSTEM EXPLAINED
Cursor Has 3 Types of Context
1. Automatic Context (Codebase Indexing)
   ✓ Indexes your entire project automatically
   ✓ Understands file structure
   ✓ Knows about all classes/functions
   ✓ NO ACTION NEEDED - happens on project open

2. Chat Context (@-mentions)
   ✓ You explicitly tag files/folders
   ✓ Gives focused context for specific questions
   ✓ YOU CONTROL what Cursor sees

3. Composer Context (Multi-file operations)
   ✓ You select files to modify
   ✓ Cursor reads those files automatically
   ✓ YOU SELECT which files to include
HOW TO GIVE CURSOR CONTEXT (Step-by-Step)
Method 1: Let Cursor Index Your Project (AUTOMATIC)
When you open a project, Cursor automatically indexes everything

bash
# 1. Open your project
cursor /path/to/verdance

# 2. Wait for indexing (bottom-right corner)
# You'll see: "Indexing codebase..." → "Indexing complete"
# Takes 10-60 seconds depending on project size

# 3. Done! Cursor now knows about:
✓ All your files
✓ All your classes
✓ All your functions
✓ Your file structure
✓ Your dependencies
```

**Check if indexing worked:**
```
In Cursor Chat, type:
"What files do I have in src/enemies/?"

If Cursor lists them correctly → Indexing worked!
If Cursor says "I don't know" → Need to manually give context
```

---

### Method 2: Use @-Mentions (EXPLICIT CONTEXT)

**The @ symbol lets you tag specific files/folders/code**

#### **@-mention a file:**
```
In Cursor Chat:

"@Goblin.js convert this enemy class to JSON format"

Cursor will:
✓ Read Goblin.js
✓ Understand its structure
✓ Give you accurate conversion
```

#### **@-mention multiple files:**
```
"Using @Goblin.js and @Orc.js as examples, 
 create a template for enemy JSON files"

Cursor reads both files and creates template
```

#### **@-mention a folder:**
```
"@src/enemies/ convert all enemy classes to JSON"

Cursor will:
✓ Read ALL files in src/enemies/
✓ Process each one
✓ Create JSON for all
```

#### **@-mention code blocks:**
```
Select some code, then:

"@{selected code} refactor this to use the DataDrivenSystem"

Cursor knows exactly what code you're talking about
```

#### **@-mention documentation:**
```
"@README.md and @ARCHITECTURE.md 
 help me understand the project structure"

Cursor reads your docs and uses them as context
Method 3: Use Cursor Composer with File Selection
Composer (Cmd+K) lets you SELECT which files to include

bash
# 1. Press Cmd+K to open Composer

# 2. Click "Add files" or type > to select files

# 3. Select files you want Cursor to know about:
   > src/scenes/GameScene.js
   > src/enemies/Goblin.js
   > src/combat/CombatSystem.js

# 4. Now ask your question
"Create a new enemy spawning system that integrates 
 with the existing CombatSystem"

Cursor has full context of those 3 files
Method 4: Create a Context File (FOR BIG CONVERSATIONS)
If Cursor doesn't have enough context, create a master context file

javascript
// PROJECT_CONTEXT.md (create this in your root)

# Verdance Project Context

## Project Overview
- Name: Verdance
- Type: 2D Tactical RPG
- Engine: Phaser 3
- Language: JavaScript
- Current Progress: Week 16 complete

## Key Features
- Sap Cycle System (3 phases: Blue, Crimson, Silver)
- Deep Sap Pool magic system
- 22 enemy types with 10 AI patterns
- 4 multi-phase boss encounters
- Equipment enhancement (10 levels)
- Skill trees (40+ skills)
- Set item bonuses
- Prestige system (20 perks, 4 tiers)

## Current File Structure
verdance/
  src/
    enemies/          (22 enemy class files)
      Goblin.js
      Orc.js
      Dragon.js
      [... 19 more]
    combat/
      CombatSystem.js
      DamageCalculator.js
    items/
      Equipment.js
      EnhancementSystem.js
    skills/
      SkillTree.js
    scenes/
      GameScene.js
      MainMenuScene.js

## Current Architecture
- Enemies: Class-based (need to migrate to data-driven)
- Combat: Hardcoded formulas (need to migrate to JSON)
- Items: Mixed approach
- Save System: Not implemented yet

## What I'm Building Now
5 core development tools:
1. DataDrivenSystem - Load data from JSON
2. SaveSystem - Save/load game state
3. PrefabSystem - Template-based GameObject creation
4. PerformanceProfiler - Monitor FPS/memory
5. AddressablesSystem - Asset loading by string address

## Example Enemy Class (Current Format)
```javascript
class Goblin extends Enemy {
    constructor(scene, x, y) {
        super(scene, x, y, 'goblin');
        this.health = 50;
        this.attack = 12;
        this.defense = 8;
        this.aiType = 'aggressive';
    }
}
```

## Target Enemy Format (After Migration)
```json
{
    "id": "goblin",
    "name": "Goblin Warrior",
    "health": 50,
    "attack": 12,
    "defense": 8,
    "ai": { "type": "aggressive" }
}
```

 Create the 5 core systems I outlined.
 Make sure they integrate with my existing architecture."

Key files you should know about:
@src/scenes/GameScene.js
@src/enemies/Goblin.js
@src/combat/CombatSystem.js"

This sets the stage for the whole conversation
```

### 2. Use @-mentions Liberally
```
Good: "Convert @Goblin.js to JSON"
Better: "Using @Goblin.js and @Orc.js as examples, 
         convert all enemies in @src/enemies/ to JSON"

Cursor gets more context = better results
```

### 3. Reference Previous Messages
```
Cursor remembers conversation history!

Message 1: "Create DataDrivenSystem.js"
[Cursor creates it]

Message 2: "Now create SaveSystem.js that works with 
            the DataDrivenSystem you just created"

Cursor knows what it created before!
```

### 4. Attach Files to Chat
```
In Cursor Chat:
- Drag and drop files directly into chat
- Or click the paperclip icon to attach files
- Cursor reads them automatically
5. Create a .cursorrules File
Cursor reads .cursorrules automatically for project-specific context

bash
# Create in project root
touch .cursorrules
```
```
// .cursorrules

This is Verdance, a Phaser 3 tactical RPG.

ARCHITECTURE PRINCIPLES:
- Data-driven design (load from JSON, not hardcode)
- Component-based entities
- Modular systems
- Save/load everything

CODE STYLE:
- ES6 modules (import/export)
- Classes for systems
- JSDoc comments
- Async/await for loading

FILE STRUCTURE:
src/
  systems/     - Core game systems
  entities/    - Game objects (Player, Enemy, NPC)
  components/  - Reusable components
  tools/       - Development tools
  data/        - JSON data files
  scenes/      - Phaser scenes

NAMING CONVENTIONS:
- Systems: PascalCase (DataDrivenSystem)
- Files: Match class name (DataDrivenSystem.js)
- Data files: lowercase (goblin.json)
- Prefabs: prefab_{name} (prefab_goblin)

CURRENT MIGRATION:
Converting from class-based enemies to data-driven prefab system.
```

**Cursor reads this automatically and follows your rules!**

---

## TROUBLESHOOTING: "CURSOR DOESN'T HAVE CONTEXT"

### Issue 1: Indexing Not Complete
```
Symptom: Cursor says "I don't have access to that file"

Fix:
1. Check bottom-right: Should say "Indexing complete"
2. If still indexing, wait for it to finish
3. If stuck, restart Cursor
4. If still broken, use @-mentions explicitly
```

### Issue 2: Large Project
```
Symptom: Cursor only sees some files, not all

Fix:
1. Cursor has limits on indexing size
2. Use @-mentions for specific files you need
3. Create PROJECT_CONTEXT.md with key info
4. Focus Cursor on relevant directories only:
   "@src/enemies/ @src/systems/ work with these"
```

### Issue 3: Cursor Gives Generic Answers
```
Symptom: Cursor's answers don't match your project

Fix:
1. Be more specific with @-mentions:
   "@GameScene.js integrate with THIS file specifically"

2. Paste relevant code directly:
   "Here's my existing code:
    [paste code]
    Now create a system that works with THIS."

3. Reference conversation history:
   "Remember the DataDrivenSystem you just created? 
    Now make SaveSystem work with it."
PERFECT CURSOR WORKFLOW FOR YOUR PROJECT
Session 1: Setup (5 minutes)
bash
# 1. Open Cursor
cursor /path/to/verdance

# 2. Wait for indexing to complete

# 3. Create context file
touch PROJECT_CONTEXT.md
[paste project overview]

# 4. Create .cursorrules
touch .cursorrules
[paste coding standards]

# 5. Verify context
In Cursor Chat: "What files exist in src/enemies/?"
If correct: ✓ Context is working!
```

### Session 2: Start Building (30 seconds)
```
Cmd+K (Composer)

"@PROJECT_CONTEXT.md

I need to create 5 core development systems.

Create:
1. src/systems/DataDrivenSystem.js
2. src/systems/SaveSystem.js
3. src/systems/PrefabSystem.js
4. src/tools/PerformanceProfiler.js
5. src/systems/AddressablesSystem.js

Here are the complete implementations:
[paste Tool 1]
[paste Tool 14]
[paste Tool 20]
[paste Tool 24]
[paste Tool 26]

Also:
- Create example data: public/data/enemies/goblin.json
- Integrate into @GameScene.js
- Add keyboard shortcuts (F1, F5, F9)"

Cursor now has FULL context:
✓ Your project overview (from @PROJECT_CONTEXT.md)
✓ Your existing GameScene.js (from @-mention)
✓ The tool implementations (from paste)
✓ Your entire codebase (from indexing)
```

### Session 3: Migration (with context)
```
In Chat:

"@src/enemies/ convert all enemy classes to JSON.

For each file:
1. Create JSON in public/data/enemies/
2. Extract stats, AI, loot tables
3. Follow this template:
[paste enemy JSON template]

Start with @Goblin.js as the example."

Cursor has context of:
✓ All 22 enemy files (from @src/enemies/)
✓ The specific example (from @Goblin.js)
✓ Your template structure (from paste)
```

## TL;DR: HOW TO GIVE CURSOR CONTEXT
```
Method 1: Let indexing work (automatic)
Method 2: Use @-mentions (@filename, @folder/)
Method 3: Create PROJECT_CONTEXT.md
Method 4: Create .cursorrules
Method 5: Paste code directly

Best Practice: Combine all methods
"@PROJECT_CONTEXT.md and @GameScene.js 
 create these systems:
 [paste implementations]"
Cursor now has MAXIMUM context!

Want me to create the complete context files (PROJECT_CONTEXT.md + .cursorrules) ready for you to paste?







Claude is AI and can make mistakes. Please double-check responses.
